title: viola jalapaensis
intro: Viola jalapaensis, also known as the wild pansy or small blue violet, is a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Viola jalapaensis include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues. Viola jalapaensis is often prepared as a tea or used in herbal infusions, and its leaves and flowers can also be used in tinctures or added to salves for topical applications.
